behavior_summary: Aramina is an NPC whose short description (U8P_ARAMINA::look) changes based on whether she is dead and on two internal name/job flags.

A U8P_ARAMINA::schedule system (araminaSchedule) selects one of several behavior branches; U8P_ARAMINA::schedule selection is performed by U8P_ARAMINA::schedule() and drive-time behavior is dispatched when the player enters the NPC's fast area (U8P_ARAMINA::enterFastArea).

Behavior branches (U8P_ARAMINA::schedule values 1, 2, 3) run distinct helper utilities (U8P_ARAMINA::func0460, U8P_ARAMINA::func0CEC, U8P_ARAMINA::func0BBB) that inspect nearby items, possibly pathfind to them, play attention/animation helpers, speak monologues, spawn timed helper processes, or manipulate items (create/pop/setFrame/destroy).

U8P_ARAMINA::use() is the full conversation handler presented when the player talks to Aramina: it builds a dynamic menu, presents choices, captures the choice, updates an 'met' flag, and emits many dialogue lines depending on U8P_ARAMINA::schedule, quest flags, and prior interactions.

U8P_ARAMINA::AvatarStoleSomething and U8P_ARAMINA::cast implement crime/combat reactions: they clear interaction refs, call a preparatory/method service, bark warning/panic lines, notify guards (CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime), and spawn helper processes for follow-up.
U8P_ARAMINA::leaveFastArea clears refs and, if Aramina is dead, teleports her to random coordinates (likely to remove or randomize corpse placement).
notable_control_flow: U8P_ARAMINA::look (process): test Npc::isDead(this) -> corpse bark; else choose label by araminaName && araminaJob -> 'servant Aramina', then araminaName -> 'Aramina', then araminaJob -> 'servant', default -> 'girl'.

U8P_ARAMINA::schedule (process): computes candidate U8P_ARAMINA::schedule (local4), calls METHOD::func143A (suspend), and on process_result() true assigns araminaSchedule = local4 and calls METHOD::func133F.
U8P_ARAMINA::enterFastArea (process): clears reference slots (resetRef) then dispatches to U8P_ARAMINA::func0460, ::func0CEC, or ::func0BBB based on araminaSchedule == 1, 2, 3 respectively.

U8P_ARAMINA::func0460 / U8P_ARAMINA::func0BBB: read Item::getFrame, optionally Npc::pathfind (suspend) and branch on pathfind/process_result; success paths choose between short bark utilities (U8P_ARAMINA::func4CF1), longer monologues (U8P_ARAMINA::func059D), or spawn helpers (FREE::func1C19) plus METHOD::func15E7.

U8P_ARAMINA::use (process): construct menu array (local3) with MenuSet/MenuUnion/MenuAdd/MenuRemove, Ask -> SuspendAssign to capture selection into local1, strcmp on local1 to handle options and loop until player chooses 'bye '.
open_questions: Are flags such as araminaMet, araminaSchedule, araminaName and araminaJob script-local variables or global script flags shared across multiple instances? (metadata shows no engine-global flags written).

What is the exact semantic meaning of the raw conditions labeled '0x0D' that gate item-create vs item-set branches in U8P_ARAMINA::func0E05/U8P_ARAMINA::func0F6F/U8P_ARAMINA::func4CF1? (appears to be an item-presence or state test).
What does ARAMINA::func01D6 (called from U8P_ARAMINA::AvatarStoleSomething) fully do? It is referenced but not included in the functions list; its exact follow-up actions are inferred only.

Precise side-effects of METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7 are inferred as 'prepare to speak / animation' but their concrete engine effects (UI vs animation vs state) are not proven in this extract.

main_function.name: U8P_ARAMINA::look
main_function.purpose: Return a short examination description for Aramina that depends on life state and local name/job flags.
entry_conditions: Player performs the 'U8P_ARAMINA::look' / examine action on the NPC.
Npc::isDead(this) determines whether to show corpse text vs living variants.
Reads script-local flags araminaName and araminaJob to choose between labelled variants.

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::look
function_map.role: short description / examine handler (shows corpse or name/job variants)

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::schedule
function_map.role: select/write araminaSchedule by querying scheduling service (METHOD::func143A) and finalize with METHOD::func133F

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::enterFastArea
function_map.role: triggered when Avatar enters NPC fast/trigger area; clears refs and dispatches U8P_ARAMINA::schedule-driven behavior

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: cleanup when Avatar leaves fast area; clears refs and teleports dead NPCs away

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: theft/crime reaction handler (clears refs, alerts, barks and delegates to follow-up handler)

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::cast
function_map.role: responds to being targeted/U8P_ARAMINA::cast upon: spawn helpers while untargeted; notify guards and bark when targeted

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::func0460
function_map.role: U8P_ARAMINA::schedule==1 behavior: inspect item frames, optionally pathfind and choose between short/long monologues or spawn helpers

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::func059D
function_map.role: larger monologue/idle-talk utility that chooses among many greeting/hostile lines and spawns helper processes

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::func0BBB
function_map.role: U8P_ARAMINA::schedule==3 behavior: frame inspection, pathfind attempts and frame-specific handlers or default spawn/animation sequence

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::func0CEC
function_map.role: U8P_ARAMINA::schedule==2 behavior: randomized short action choice between METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7 and spawn follow-up helper

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::func0E05
function_map.role: auxiliary item-frame manipulation or item create/pop with animation steps and spawn helper process

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::func0F6F
function_map.role: support routine for item drop/create/destroy sequences with timed helper spawns and animations

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::use
function_map.role: conversation/dialog handler: builds dynamic menu, captures player's choice, presents dialogue lines and sets araminaMet when introduced

function_map.name: U8P_ARAMINA::func4CF1
function_map.role: short monologue/door interaction helper ('Why is this open?') and spawn DOOR_NS::useDoor

internal_calls.purpose: Invoke longer monologue/idle-talk when pathfind succeeds and conditions allow (nobodyTalking)
Invoke short bark/door-handling when specific frame values (0 or 3) encountered after successful pathfind
Handle item frame 0x0B specific short interaction
Handle item frame 0x0F specific interaction (frame update or item create path)
Follow-up theft handling (state changes / aggression / guard notification) — referenced but function body not present in listing

external_calls.purpose: Query scheduling service that can suspend and return a boolean to accept candidate U8P_ARAMINA::schedule
Finalize or apply the chosen U8P_ARAMINA::schedule after successful query
Prepare NPC for speaking/attention before barking in theft response
Order NPC to move toward item/target and suspend until pathfinding completes
Test the outcome of suspended pathfind or other SERVICE calls to branch on success/failure
Spawn a helper/timed process for asynchronous follow-up or cleanup
Prepare NPC to speak/act prior to monologue lines
Play an animation/attention helper between barks or as alternate action
Notify guard system of a crime/combat incident
Spawn a door-U8P_ARAMINA::use action after the NPC barks about an open door
Update an item's frame/appearance in the then-branch
Create and place an item in the else-branch when no existing item is present
Destroy referenced item in one branch of the behavior sequence
Create/place an item in the alternate branch
Spawn helper/timed processes during conversation-driven sequences or follow-ups (used across many utilities called from conversation paths)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Aramina is an NPC whose short description (U8P_ARAMINA::look) changes based on whether she is dead and on two internal name/job flags.
